title: create-spec-driven-app — the day-to-day loop, with an AI agent writing code
note: One segment per section of demo.tape, in order. The build script inserts a 2.5s gap between segments so the narration tracks the screen.

segments.text: Spec-driven delivery. Specifications lead, an AI agent implements, and a hard gate decides whether the work is good enough to merge. Here is the whole loop in under two minutes.

Step one: scaffold the project from a small YAML config — project name, stack, testing tools. Then pull in a versioned domain pack, the npm-install of spec-driven development. The pack ships requirements, scenarios, and the traceability matrix that ties everything back to code.

Step two: the plan command reads the matrix and tells you, per requirement, exactly what is missing. This is the task queue an AI agent consumes. Today: implement REQ-000, the platform health endpoint.

Step three: configure the harness. Point it at any AI coding agent — opencode, Claude, or, here, a deterministic stub — and at the test command that will gate the result. The harness is vendor-neutral by design.

Step four — the loop. csda harness run picks up REQ-000, builds a self-contained prompt from the Gherkin scenario plus the project's stack rules, hands it to the agent in an isolated git worktree, and waits for the gate. The agent writes a test, then the production code; the harness runs validate and the test command; on green it closes the matrix and commits on a fresh harness branch.
Step five: look at what actually got committed. The agent's production code and its test — real files on a reviewable branch. No copy-pasting from a chat window.

Step six: run the same gate by hand. The tests pass for real, the requirement is now marked Implemented in the traceability matrix, and the harness branch is yours to review and merge — or throw away.
Specs as executable contracts. Real code, real tests, every commit reviewable. The full step-by-step guide is in docs slash tutorial dot md. Find it on GitHub and on npm as create-spec-driven-app.
